metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document an explicit restore procedure for the capped live heal (e.g. Jira admin import from JSON, or 'this deletion is irreversible — escalate to Jira admin'), or narrow the heal scope to exclude irreversible deletions until a restore path is confirmed.
findings.finding: The capped live heal describes a pre-heal backup (Jira JSON export + branch tag) and states 'heal aborts if either backup fails,' but provides no restore procedure. T4 requires an explicit rollback/back-out plan — acknowledgement of destructive intent and existence of a backup artifact are insufficient. The GC-after-grace deletion (f8b5-2f30 → REB-530) is irreversible at the Jira level (deleted issues cannot be restored from a JSON export via standard Jira API), and the plan does not describe how to use the backup to recover.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — 'Capped live heal' paragraph and AC[6]

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `baseline_consumer_swap: bool = False` to `OutboundDiffConfig` (outbound_differ.py:242) and thread it through `compute_outbound_mutations` → `_compute_outbound_update_mutation` → `_diff_fields`; name `OutboundDiffConfig` explicitly in the Scope edit list.
findings.finding: OutboundDiffConfig (outbound_differ.py:242–273) is the established channel for carrying optional flags through compute_outbound_mutations → _compute_outbound_update_mutation → _diff_fields, but the plan does not name it as an edit target or describe adding a baseline_consumer_swap field to it. The flag must traverse this dataclass to reach _diff_fields for external callers (run_differs.py:413 already constructs an OutboundDiffConfig); omitting it means either the flag is inaccessible from the orchestration layer or a separate ambient config-read is silently assumed.
findings.location: Remaining CODE §2 / Scope (files EDITED) / outbound_differ.py:242–273
findings.suggested_fix: Add `baseline_consumer_swap: bool = False` to OutboundDiffConfig alongside the existing optional fields. In run_differs.py, read the flag once (mirroring the baseline_dual_write pattern in reconcile.py:936) and pass it via OutboundDiffConfig. _diff_fields gains a `baseline_consumer_swap: bool = False` keyword parameter.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `OutboundDiffConfig` (outbound_differ.py:242) needs a `baseline_consumer_swap: bool = False` field, and that the orchestration layer (`run_differs.py` or `reconcile.py`) passes it when constructing `OutboundDiffConfig` — mirroring how `prev_snapshot` already travels through this struct to `_diff_fields`.
findings.finding: The `baseline_consumer_swap` flag has no specified propagation path through the `OutboundDiffConfig` interface boundary. The plan says to thread the bool 'down' from orchestration (mirroring `reconcile.py:936`), but `compute_outbound_mutations` accepts only `OutboundDiffConfig` (outbound_differ.py:388-393). The flag must cross that boundary to reach `_compute_outbound_update_mutation` → `_diff_fields`, yet the plan never says to add it to `OutboundDiffConfig` or describes any other crossing mechanism. Without this, an implementer following the plan literally will have the flag in `_diff_fields`'s signature but no way to plumb it from `reconcile.py` through `compute_outbound_mutations`.
findings.location: Remaining CODE item 2 ('Plumbing: thread `local_id` (already in scope at `outbound_differ.py:622`) into the `_diff_fields` call … plus the `baseline_consumer_swap` bool')
findings.suggested_fix: Add `baseline_consumer_swap: bool = False` to `OutboundDiffConfig`. In `reconcile.py` (or wherever `OutboundDiffConfig` is constructed for the live pass), set `baseline_consumer_swap=bool(_load_config().reconciler.baseline_consumer_swap)` — exactly one config read, mirroring the `baseline_dual_write` pattern at reconcile.py:936.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] [T1] Add a code citation (file:line) to the plan confirming that `baseline_shadow_check` is routed to stdout (not exclusively to the ephemeral JSONL log), e.g., by citing the logger call in `baseline_shadow.py` and confirming it uses `print()`/`logging` to stdout rather than solely `SyncLogger`.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `baseline_shadow_check` values are emitted to stdout (captured by GHA logs and therefore retrievable via `gh run view --log`) rather than only to the ephemeral `sync-log-*.jsonl` SyncLogger file, but this is stated as fact without a code citation verifying that the reconciler routes this specific log key to stdout.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — 'the reconcile-bridge run already prints `baseline_shadow_check` … to stdout, which GitHub Actions captures durably'; also Key facts — 'baseline_shadow.py:91-95'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `baseline_consumer_swap` is added as a new field to `OutboundDiffConfig` (mirroring how `prev_snapshot` is carried), threaded from `reconcile.py` → `run_differs.py` (at the `OutboundDiffConfig(...)` instantiation site, ~line 413) → `compute_outbound_mutations` → `_compute_outbound_update_mutation` → `_diff_fields`.
findings.finding: The plan directs threading `baseline_consumer_swap` bool into `_diff_fields` but never specifies adding it to `OutboundDiffConfig` — the established config-dataclass channel for optional flags into `compute_outbound_mutations` → `_compute_outbound_update_mutation` → `_diff_fields`. As written, a builder must infer whether to add a new field to `OutboundDiffConfig` (which would require updating `run_differs.py:413`'s `OutboundDiffConfig(...)` instantiation, and `reconcile.py`'s threading into `config`) or to bypass the dataclass and pass the flag through a parallel route. The protocol for where the flag lives in the call chain is unspecified.
findings.location: Remaining CODE item 2 / outbound_differ.py:242-273 (OutboundDiffConfig) / run_differs.py:413
findings.suggested_fix: Add `baseline_consumer_swap: bool = False` to `OutboundDiffConfig` (outbound_differ.py:273, after `dropped_field_sink`). In `run_differs.py` at the `OutboundDiffConfig(...)` call (~line 413), pass `baseline_consumer_swap=_baseline_consumer_swap` (the bool read once from config at orchestration layer). No change to `_diff_fields`'s positional signature needed — access it via `config.baseline_consumer_swap` if config is passed, or accept it as an explicit kwarg.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fourth streak-reset condition: a `gh run view --log | grep baseline_shadow_check` that returns no match (empty) for a run with `conclusion=success` RESETS the streak (treat as equivalent to divergent>0) — document this explicitly in the streak derivation procedure.
findings.finding: The streak reset rule specifies 'a gap > 2× the schedule interval RESETS the streak' but does not specify a fallback for a `gh run view --log | grep baseline_shadow_check` that returns no matches (empty grep result) — which occurs when a GHA run aborted before the shadow-check executed, or when the log was truncated. The plan specifies reset for `divergent>0` and for `failed/absent run`, but the no-match grep case is neither: the run may show `conclusion=success` yet have no `baseline_shadow_check` line in its log.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL section — streak derivation procedure
findings.suggested_fix: In the streak derivation prose, add: 'A run whose log contains no `baseline_shadow_check` line, regardless of its `conclusion` field, is treated as divergent>0 and resets the streak. Only a line matching `baseline_shadow_check` with `divergent=0` (or `"divergent": 0`) counts as a clean pass.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an [OPERATIONAL] AC: after flipping `baseline_consumer_swap=true`, run ≥1 live reconcile-bridge pass with the flag ON and confirm `baseline_shadow_divergence==0` and zero unexpected outbound mutations (record the run-id as proof); this exercises the flag=ON path end-to-end as it runs in production.
findings.finding: After the flag is flipped to ON in production, no AC exercises the `baseline_consumer_swap=true` code path end-to-end (live reconciler pass) and verifies observable behavior (e.g., zero unexpected outbound mutations, reconciler reaches stable state). All shadow-pass validation runs with flag=OFF; the only post-flip check is a dry-run with flag REVERTED. Per E6-f, a cutover to a new code path requires at least one AC that exercises that path as it will run in production — green offline tests plus a signed completion verdict are not sufficient.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — [OPERATIONAL] Flip only after both gates; REMAINING OPERATIONAL section
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the flag-flip AC: 'After flip, run one full live reconciler pass with baseline_consumer_swap=true; assert baseline_shadow_check.divergent==0 in that run's GHA log (gh run view <id> --log | grep baseline_shadow_check). Record the run-id. Only then mark the flip complete.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the exact pre-heal Jira backup mechanism (e.g., `client.get_issue_by_rest(key)?fields=summary,description,priority,status,assignee,comment,issuelinks` for each affected issue key, or a script wrapping it), confirm the field set covers all fields the live heal may mutate, and explicitly wire the 'abort if backup fails' gate to that invocation's exit/exception.
findings.finding: The pre-heal bilateral backup asserts 'export affected Jira issue state (JSON via Jira API)' but names no specific method, endpoint variant, or script. The default `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}` (the only Jira REST GET method present in the codebase, `acli.py:363-371`) omits comments and issuelinks from its response unless `?fields=...` or `expand` parameters are added — meaning the backup as described is PARTIALLY-VERIFIED for basic fields only and unverified for full-state coverage needed for a meaningful restore. Additionally, the 'abort if either backup fails' gate cannot be implemented without a named invocation to gate on.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — 'Capped live heal' bullet: 'PRE-HEAL BACKUP covers BOTH sides: export affected Jira issue state (JSON via Jira API) AND tag the tickets branch'; AC '[OPERATIONAL] Capped live heal with a bilateral pre-heal backup'
findings.suggested_fix: In the OPERATIONAL section, replace 'export affected Jira issue state (JSON via Jira API)' with a specific call: e.g., 'for each affected Jira key, call `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}?fields=summary,description,priority,status,assignee,comment,issuelinks` and persist the response as `pre-heal-<key>-<date>.json` in the workflow run artifacts; abort if any GET returns non-2xx.' Update the AC to require that the backup artifact list is non-empty before the heal begins.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify the exact `gh run list --json` field names (`databaseId`, `createdAt`, `conclusion`) and `gh run view --log` flag against `gh run list --help` / `gh run view --help` before the operational window; record the observed flag output in the ticket as evidence, and add a non-zero-match assertion (require ≥1 `baseline_shadow_check` line per run before counting it as clean) to prevent a silent grep miss from masquerading as a clean pass.
findings.finding: The streak-derivation procedure uses `gh run list --workflow=reconcile-bridge.yml --limit N --json databaseId,createdAt,conclusion` followed by `gh run view <id> --log | grep baseline_shadow_check`. The existing codebase only uses `gh api repos/.../actions/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml/runs?...` (not `gh run list --json`) for workflow-run queries (reconcile-bridge-canary.yml:134-136). The specific `--json` field names `databaseId`, `createdAt`, `conclusion` and the `--log` flag on `gh run view` are not confirmed against `--help` output in the repo — they are UNVERIFIED at flag-level granularity. A flag-name mismatch (e.g., `databaseId` vs `id`, or `--log` outputting structured JSON in newer gh versions) would silently break the streak gate.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — '≥10 CONSECUTIVE clean shadow passes' streak derivation: 'gh run list --workflow=reconcile-bridge.yml --limit N --json databaseId,createdAt,conclusion' and 'gh run view <id> --log | grep baseline_shadow_check'
findings.suggested_fix: Run `gh run list --help` and `gh run view --help` in the operational environment before the streak window, confirm the exact field names and flag spelling, and update the OPERATIONAL section. Add the assertion: 'A run with zero `baseline_shadow_check` lines in its log RESETS the streak (not counts as clean) — to guard against grep finding no output due to log-format changes.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the concrete cap for the live heal (e.g., 'at most N issues per run, derived from check_blast_radius default 0.10 × total active bindings') in the OPERATIONAL section and the AC so the criterion is measurable.
findings.finding: The 'capped live heal' never states the cap (maximum number of issues to heal in one pass). 'Capped' is used as a scope-limiting claim, but no numeric limit or derivation formula is given, making it impossible to evaluate whether the AC is met or to set a blast-radius threshold.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — 'Capped live heal' bullet; Acceptance Criteria — [OPERATIONAL] Capped live heal AC

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the config-parse test (TOML round-trip for baseline_consumer_swap) and the corrupt-store abort test to the 'Remaining CODE' items and 'Scope (files EDITED)', specifying their file locations.
findings.finding: The 'Scope (files EDITED)' section lists only one new test file ('new test file under tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/ — RED-first direction-preservation property test'), but AC[0] requires a config-parse test (default-False + TOML round-trip) and AC[1] requires a corrupt-store abort test. These two additional tests have no corresponding entry in the scope list or in the 'Remaining CODE' items.
findings.location: Scope (files EDITED) vs. AC[0] ('a config-parse test asserts default False AND that a TOML baseline_consumer_swap = true round-trips') and AC[1] ('a test asserts a corrupt store aborts the pass')
findings.suggested_fix: In Code item 1, add: 'Test: add a config-parse test (e.g. tests/unit/test_config_schema.py) asserting default=False and TOML round-trip.' In Code item 2, add: 'Test: add a corrupt-store test (e.g. tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_binding_store.py) asserting _load raises ValueError on a corrupt bindings.json.' Add both files to Scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the `run_differs.py` Scope entry to remove 'scoped divergence alert' (replace with 'census expand-contract only') so it matches CODE item 5's explicit 'No new alert code' decision.
findings.finding: The Scope (files EDITED) entry for run_differs.py reads 'census expand-contract; scoped divergence alert', but CODE item 5 explicitly states 'This item is a DOCUMENTATION step … No new alert code.' These two sections are contradictory: the Scope description implies a behavioral code change (a new alert) that the plan's own body says will not be written.
findings.location: Scope (files EDITED) — run_differs.py line / CODE item 5
findings.suggested_fix: Change the run_differs.py scope line to: 'src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/run_differs.py — census expand-contract (emit decision_census, drop binding_walk_census)'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm explicitly in the plan that the new `local_id` and `baseline_consumer_swap` params added to `_diff_fields` carry default values (`local_id: str = ""`, `baseline_consumer_swap: bool = False`) so the ~25 existing direct call-sites in tests require no update.
findings.finding: The plan adds new parameters (local_id and baseline_consumer_swap) to _diff_fields, but does not specify that these parameters carry default values. _diff_fields is directly called in approximately 25 test files (e.g. test_inbound_field_sync_directionality.py, test_outbound_differ_adf_extraction.py, test_diff_assignee_unmappable_converges.py, etc.) without going through compute_outbound_mutations. Non-defaulted parameters would break all these direct callers.
findings.location: Remaining CODE §2 / outbound_fields.py:333 (_diff_fields signature)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to CODE §2: 'Both new _diff_fields parameters carry defaults (local_id: str = "", baseline_consumer_swap: bool = False) to preserve all existing direct call-sites unchanged.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify whether `local_id` reaches `_diff_fields` via (a) a new keyword parameter added to its signature (and enumerate which test call-sites must be updated) or (b) derivation from `ticket.get('ticket_id')` already computed inside `_diff_fields` — remove the ambiguity so the builder doesn't have to choose.
findings.finding: `_diff_fields` has no `local_id` parameter in its current signature (outbound_fields.py:333-342), yet the plan says to call `binding_store.get_baseline(local_id)` inside it. The plan says 'thread `local_id` into the `_diff_fields` call' but never explicitly states that `local_id` must be added as a new parameter to `_diff_fields`'s signature. A builder could alternatively derive it from `ticket.get('ticket_id')` (which `_diff_fields` already does at line 369 as `ticket_id`). The plan leaves this decision undefined, creating an ambiguous structural gap in the function's schema.
findings.location: Remaining CODE item 2 / outbound_fields.py:333-342 (_diff_fields signature)
findings.suggested_fix: Prefer option (b): inside `_diff_fields`, derive `local_id` from the already-computed `ticket_id` local (line 369) and call `binding_store.get_baseline(ticket_id)` — zero signature change, zero test-site breakage. State this explicitly in the plan's CODE item 2 plumbing description.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether the capped live heal is idempotent/re-entrant (e.g., confirm bootstrap-strict mode tracks processed items, or add a pre-heal checkpoint that makes re-running safe after interruption at any point including post-GC-deletion).
findings.finding: The capped live heal (including GC-after-grace deletion for REB-530) does not specify re-entrancy/idempotency: if the heal is interrupted mid-run (GHA runner killed, network timeout after some mutations are applied), it is unspecified whether re-running the heal is safe or will double-apply mutations or double-delete items.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL section — 'Capped live heal' bullet; AC '[OPERATIONAL] Capped live heal with a bilateral pre-heal backup'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document (or implement) that the capped live heal's GC-after-grace deletion step is idempotent on re-run — e.g., assert the GC pass skips already-absent binding entries gracefully and that Jira-side updates are conditional (check-then-act) — and specify the batching/cap limit so re-runs are safe.
findings.finding: The plan specifies a 'capped live heal' including GC-after-grace deletion but does not assert that the GC deletion step is idempotent or resumable. If the heal run is interrupted after partially deleting entries (or partially updating binding state), re-running may double-apply or skip entries inconsistently, with no stated mechanism to detect or handle already-processed records.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL section — 'Capped live heal' bullet; AC '[OPERATIONAL] Capped live heal with a bilateral pre-heal backup (Jira JSON + tickets-branch tag); heal aborts if either backup fails'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate known consumers of `binding_walk_census` (or explicitly state 'no external consumers confirmed beyond run_differs.py:652') so the migrate step is a concrete action, not an open-ended search.
findings.finding: The census expand-contract step says 'migrate any consumer to `decision_census`' without enumerating known consumers or stating that no consumers currently exist. This is a placeholder-like deferral ('check if there are consumers') that requires the executor to perform undirected discovery.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — item 4; Acceptance Criteria — [CODE] Census emits `decision_census`
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line: 'Known consumers of binding_walk_census: [run_differs.py:652 emit only / or list dashboards/alerts]. If none, state explicitly. Proving command: grep -rn "binding_walk_census" . after the drop to confirm zero remaining references.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the capped live heal pauses the reconciler and resets the shadow-pass streak, and add an explicit rule (analogous to the A3 reset rule) to the OPERATIONAL section.
findings.finding: The streak-reset rule mentions only A3's live remediation as a reset trigger, but the capped live heal is also a live operational step that would pause the reconciler. If the heal pauses the reconciler mid-streak, the streak gate and the heal gate could silently interact: an implementer could complete the live heal (which resets the streak), then immediately flip the flag without re-accumulating 10 clean passes.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — shadow streak rule ('A3's live remediation pauses the reconciler and RESETS this streak') vs. capped live heal step
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence after the A3 reset note: 'The capped live heal also pauses the reconciler and RESETS the streak — complete the heal before starting the streak accumulation window.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] [T7/T5e] Before dropping `binding_walk_census`, add an explicit consumer-inventory step to the plan (e.g., `grep -r binding_walk_census` across monitoring configs, alert rules, CI fixtures, and any external repos) and require that all found consumers are migrated before the drop AC is marked complete.
findings.finding: The plan drops `binding_walk_census` without requiring a prior inventory of downstream consumers (dashboards, alert rules, external scripts, CI log-grep fixtures) that key on it — silent breakage is possible.
findings.location: Remaining CODE item 4 ('DROP `binding_walk_census`') and AC ('a test asserts `decision_census` is emitted and `binding_walk_census` is not')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a pre-drop checklist item: 'Run `grep -r "binding_walk_census"` across the monorepo, alert configs, and any downstream consumers; migrate each; document findings on the ticket.' Gate the drop on this audit completing.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] [T7] Name a specific documentation target (e.g., a section in `docs/jira-sync-setup.md` or a code comment block in `outbound_fields.py`) for the `_INBOUND_MIRRORED_FIELDS` / title-vs-summary asymmetry, and add it to the Scope list so the obligation is not dropped.
findings.finding: The plan directs 'Document it' for the `_INBOUND_MIRRORED_FIELDS` / title-vs-summary asymmetry but names no target file in the Scope list — the documentation destination is unspecified, so an implementer could reasonably omit it.
findings.location: Key facts section ('Document it; do NOT silently fix…') vs. Scope (files EDITED list)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `## Field-suppression asymmetry` subsection to `docs/jira-sync-setup.md` (already in Scope) explaining why `"summary"` is not in `_INBOUND_MIRRORED_FIELDS` and that this is intentional/unchanged by the swap. Reference it from the property test's docstring.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before dropping `binding_walk_census`, add an explicit audit step to enumerate operational consumers (dashboards, alert rules, log-based metrics) that key on that string, and confirm each is migrated or removed prior to the drop.
findings.finding: The census key rename from `binding_walk_census` to `decision_census` instructs 'migrate any consumer' but does not enumerate external/operational consumers (e.g., Grafana dashboards, on-call alert rules, log-scraping queries) that may key on the `binding_walk_census` string — only code consumers are implicitly covered by the codebase search.
findings.location: Remaining CODE item 4 (Census expand-contract); AC '[CODE] Census emits `decision_census`; consumers migrated; `binding_walk_census` dropped'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a pre-drop checklist item: `grep -r 'binding_walk_census' monitoring/ dashboards/ alerts/` (or equivalent for your observability stack) and document zero-result or list of updated artifacts in the commit / ticket evidence.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either (a) move the census expand-contract (item 4 / AC line 4) to a separate, lower-risk ticket so the three title promises are the sole deliverables of this plan, OR (b) retitle the plan to include '+ census key migration' and enumerate the specific consumers of `binding_walk_census` that must be migrated before the DROP is safe.
findings.finding: The plan's title promises exactly three deliverables — (1) Phase-3 consumer swap flip, (2) live shadow-pass validation, and (3) capped live heal — but the body adds a fourth, independently-scoped work item: the 'census expand-contract' (drop `binding_walk_census`, emit `decision_census`, migrate consumers). This item has its own AC, its own test assertion, and a documented rollback path, making it a distinct scope addition not implied by any of the three title promises. This is scope drift (doing MORE than the title promises) and risks conflating two unrelated risk profiles in the same rollout window.
findings.location: Remaining CODE item 4; AC line 4 ('[CODE] Census emits `decision_census`; consumers migrated; `binding_walk_census` dropped')
findings.suggested_fix: Carve item 4 and its AC into a standalone ticket (e.g., a follow-on to this one). The census rename carries no live-heal risk and is independently releasable; keeping it separate lets rollback of the consumer swap be a clean single-variable operation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] [G5] Consider extracting the census expand-contract (rename `binding_walk_census` → `decision_census` + consumer migration + drop) into a separate ticket that ships independently of the consumer swap, since it is not on the critical path of the swap validation and carries its own rollback/failure mode.
findings.finding: The census expand-contract (item 4: rename `binding_walk_census` → `decision_census`, migrate consumers, drop the old key) is a separable observability concern bundled into this swap ticket. It has its own AC, its own test assertion, its own rollback path, and its own 'reason to change' (census/logging schema vs. arbitration-logic correctness). It is NOT on the critical path of the consumer swap — the divergence gate uses `baseline_shadow_check`, not `decision_census`. It could ship before or after the swap independently without coupling.
findings.location: Remaining CODE item 4; AC line '[CODE] Census emits `decision_census`'; Scope file `run_differs.py`
findings.suggested_fix: Move items 4 and its AC ('Census emits decision_census; consumers migrated; binding_walk_census dropped') to a thin follow-on ticket. This ticket retains items 1–3 + 5 + operational gates. The census ticket can ship before or after the flag flip without any ordering dependency.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete anchored proving command for the dry-run zero-mutations claim, e.g.: `gh run view <id> --log | grep -E '"mutations_proposed"\s*:\s*0'` (or the actual structured log key emitted by dry-run mode).
findings.finding: The rollback-rehearsal AC specifies 'force one `--mode dry-run` pass confirming zero mutations' but provides no structured proving command that asserts zero mutations from the dry-run output. 'Confirming zero mutations' is a prose claim without an anchored grep/jq assertion, leaving it vulnerable to a false-positive (a run that prints mutations but isn't checked).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — [OPERATIONAL] Flip only after both gates

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] [T5e] Either add a new ADR or append a `## Phase-3 consumer swap` amendment to ADR 0026 documenting that active-binding field suppression now reads `get_baseline` (behind `baseline_consumer_swap`) instead of `prev_snapshot`, and why.
findings.finding: The consumer swap introduces a new arbitration source for active-binding field suppression (replacing `prev_snapshot` with `get_baseline`) — a meaningful architectural shift — but no ADR update or new ADR entry is planned, even though the system demonstrably uses ADRs (ADR 0026 §2 is referenced).
findings.location: Remaining CODE item 2; Key facts ('No field-name translation is required for the swap'); Acceptance Criteria (CODE item 2)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] [T1] Justify the grep-based streak approach over the established GHA repository-variable pattern (`gh variable set/get`), or add a non-zero-match assertion (e.g., require at least one `baseline_shadow_check` line per run before counting it clean) to prevent silent grep failures from masquerading as clean passes.
findings.finding: The plan's operational streak-verification mechanism (grepping GHA run logs via `gh run view <id> --log | grep baseline_shadow_check`) is a novel, grep-fragile approach chosen without considering the established GHA-native alternative of repository/environment variables (`gh variable set/get`) for durable cross-run counters — which requires zero app code and zero workflow edits.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL section — '≥10 CONSECUTIVE clean shadow passes' streak derivation; AC [OPERATIONAL] bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the OPERATIONAL section: 'Each run must emit ≥1 `baseline_shadow_check` line (verify with a non-empty grep result) before being counted clean — a run with zero matches is treated as UNKNOWN and resets the streak.' This is sufficient to close the silent-failure gap without new code.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the pre-heal backup to explicitly snapshot `bindings.json` (e.g., `cp bridge_state/bindings.json bridge_state/bindings.pre-heal-<date>.json` stored as a workflow artifact) BEFORE any GC deletion, and document that rollback restores this file — not just the git tag — so post-rollback local-store state is fully defined.
findings.finding: The `bindings.json` local store is gitignored (`bridge_state/` per `.gitignore:10`, as the plan itself cites), so the specified `git tag pre-heal-<date>` backup does NOT capture it. The bilateral backup covers only Jira JSON + the tickets branch. If the capped live heal partially mutates `bindings.json` (e.g., GC-after-grace deletion removes some entries) and then fails, the rollback path has no restorable snapshot of the local store, leaving it in an indeterminate intermediate state.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL section — 'Capped live heal' bullet; AC '[OPERATIONAL] Capped live heal with a bilateral pre-heal backup'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a third backup step: copy `bindings.json` to a timestamped path and upload it as a workflow artifact alongside the Jira JSON export. Update the rollback procedure to explicitly restore this file as step 1 before re-tagging. Update the AC to require this artifact is linked on the ticket.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] [T5c / AUTHN+SECRET] Declare the access posture for all boundary-crossing credentials in `docs/jira-sync-setup.md`: specify that Jira API token and GitHub token are stored as GHA encrypted secrets (not plaintext in config/logs), identify the minimum required Jira permission scope (e.g., project-scoped write, not site-admin), and confirm no token value appears in workflow logs or artifact output.
findings.finding: The live Jira API calls (live heal mutations + bilateral pre-heal backup export) and GHA `gh` CLI calls cross a network boundary, but the plan never declares the access posture of the credentials used: token type (API token vs. OAuth), storage location (GHA secret vs. plaintext), and permission scope are all unspecified. An undeclared access posture on a boundary-crossing path is itself the failure under T5c.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — 'Capped live heal' section; AC [OPERATIONAL] bullet 2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Credential contract' subsection to `docs/jira-sync-setup.md` that names each secret (e.g., `JIRA_API_TOKEN`, `GH_TOKEN`), its storage location (`Settings > Secrets and variables > Actions`), its minimum required Jira permission scope (project-level read+write on the target project only), and an explicit statement that the heal step must not echo token values to stdout/logs.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] [T5c / DATA-AT-REST] In `docs/jira-sync-setup.md`, declare the artifact access posture: confirm the repository is private (or document the consequence if public), specify the retention period (e.g., 30 days), and note whether Jira JSON exports are screened for PII/sensitive custom fields before storage or whether that is an accepted risk.
findings.finding: The pre-heal backup stores Jira issue JSON as GHA workflow run artifacts, but the plan does not declare the access posture of these artifacts (who can read them, retention window) nor whether the exported Jira JSON may contain PII or sensitive custom fields that warrant sanitization before storage. The plan says only 'retention documented' without specifying what that documentation says.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — 'Capped live heal' section; AC [OPERATIONAL] bullet 2 ('artifacts stored + linked')

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
